methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Rivet Industries is a seed-stage company operating in Defense & Security, a sector with 19 identified peers spanning public and private markets. The comparable universe includes 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms, with top-tier private peers including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems.

The peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage comparables. Of the 19 Defense & Security peers identified, zero companies matched both seed stage and similar valuation tier criteria. Only one adjacent-stage peer was located, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required for meaningful statistical triangulation. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce excessive noise given Rivet's specialized subsector positioning. This constraint materially limits the robustness of quantitative valuation anchoring.

Without sufficient narrow-peer data, a defensible valuation range cannot be derived from this analysis alone. The key uncertainty is the lack of pricing transparency at seed stage within Defense & Security, where deal flow remains limited and valuations highly founder/investor-dependent. Practitioners evaluating Rivet should pursue alternative methods: founder pedigree assessment, TAM-based modeling, or direct reference checks with investors in the identified peer set. The existence of well-capitalized private competitors (Anduril, Helsing, Shield AI) indicates sector maturity and investor appetite, but provides no direct calibration for an early-stage entrant.
